context: Last full week before the Jun 15–28 break. The threshold session missed in week 8 carries forward to Tuesday. Make Tue and Thu count — these are the stimulus sessions that consolidate during rest. Easy days must stay Z2 (129–149 bpm); your 4-week Z3 rate is 22%, which is dragging recovery.

sessions.label: Easy 6mi
sessions.detail: Z2 · 129–149 bpm. If HR drifts above 150, walk until it drops back. Your recent easy runs are averaging Z3 — today sets the tone for the week.

sessions.label: Threshold Tempo 38min
sessions.detail: WU 10min to Z2 · 38min continuous Z4 (162–178 bpm) · CD to Z2. Carried forward from week 8. Start at 162–165, let HR climb naturally to 175–178 by the final 10min. Don’t spike — drift is fine.

sessions.label: Easy 5mi
sessions.detail: Z1 · under 135 bpm. True recovery after Tuesday’s tempo. Go slower than feels right. If you ran the tempo hard, this should feel embarrassingly easy.

sessions.label: Easy 5mi + Strides + 4×200m
sessions.detail: 5mi Z2 · 4×20s strides (brief Z5+ spikes, that’s fine) · 4×200m at Z5+ (189–195 bpm) w/ 2min walk recovery. Each 200m should take ~50–60s. HR won’t fully stabilise — these are neuromuscular, not aerobic. Focus on turnover, not time.

sessions.label: Full Rest
sessions.detail: Off. Two hard sessions this week — the rest is part of the training.

sessions.label: Easy 6mi
sessions.detail: Z2 · 129–149 bpm. Legs should feel decent after the rest day. Keep it honest — no tempo creep.

sessions.label: Long Run 9mi
sessions.detail: Z2 · 135–151 bpm. Last long run before the break. Comfortable conversational effort throughout. Slight HR drift in the final 2mi is acceptable. Finish strong but not spent.

notes: This is the final training week before two weeks off. The break is planned and earned — don’t skip sessions to ‘pre-rest’ and don’t add mileage to ‘bank’ fitness. Hit Tuesday and Thursday, run the easy days easy, finish the long run Sunday. The rest period will consolidate everything.
